exam.title: IC1 Section A pilot
notes: Marks are provisional until teacher review.

rubric.question: Using the information above, identify two factors that may affect PES. [2]
mark_scheme_points: Time period / new trees take years to produce.
Availability of suitable land, labour or fertiliser.
award_rules: Award one mark per valid identification.
Do not require explanation for an identify question.

rubric.question: Explain one advantage of a market economic system. [4]
mark_scheme_points: Profit incentive encourages firms to produce what consumers want.
Competition may improve quality or efficiency.
Resources are allocated toward goods with higher demand.
award_rules: Do not award vague references to businesses making money unless linked to allocation or consumer demand.

answers.evidence: The answer identifies time needed for trees to grow.
answers.missing_point: No second valid PES factor identified.
answers.transcription: new trees take a long time

unmatched.reason: Student name is cropped or unreadable.

run_notes: Teacher review is required before final marks are used.
limitations: Diagram answers require manual review unless all labels and shifts are clearly readable.
